=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (5:3-5:15) TS377095: `Effect.orElseSucceed` expresses the same recovery more directly than `Effect.catch` followed by `Effect.succeed`. effect(catchToOrElseSucceed)
  Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed"

[D2] (10:3-10:15) TS377095: `Effect.orElseSucceed` expresses the same recovery more directly than `Effect.catch` followed by `Effect.succeed`. effect(catchToOrElseSucceed)
  Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed"

[D3] (18:3-18:15) TS377095: `Effect.orElseSucceed` expresses the same recovery more directly than `Effect.catch` followed by `Effect.succeed`. effect(catchToOrElseSucceed)
  Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed"

[D4] (23:3-23:15) TS377095: `Effect.orElseSucceed` expresses the same recovery more directly than `Effect.catch` followed by `Effect.succeed`. effect(catchToOrElseSucceed)
  Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed" ===

--- file:///.src/catchToOrElseSucceed.ts ---
import { Effect, pipe } from "effect"

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed
export const shouldTriggerArrow = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.orElseSucceed(() => 42)
)

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed (block body)
export const shouldTriggerArrowBlock = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should trigger: pipe style
export const shouldTriggerPipe = pipe(
  Effect.fail("error"),
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed("fallback"))
)

// Should trigger: with function expression
export const shouldTriggerFunctionExpr = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(function() {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should NOT trigger: callback receives the error value
export const shouldNotTriggerWithErrorParam = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch((cause) => Effect.succeed(cause))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch returning a different Effect constructor
export const shouldNotTriggerOtherEffect = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.sync(() => "fallback"))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch with logic other than just Effect.succeed
export const shouldNotTriggerWithLogic = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    console.log("recovering")
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed" ===

--- file:///.src/catchToOrElseSucceed.ts ---
import { Effect, pipe } from "effect"

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed
export const shouldTriggerArrow = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(42))
)

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed (block body)
export const shouldTriggerArrowBlock = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.orElseSucceed(() => {
    return 42
  })
)

// Should trigger: pipe style
export const shouldTriggerPipe = pipe(
  Effect.fail("error"),
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed("fallback"))
)

// Should trigger: with function expression
export const shouldTriggerFunctionExpr = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(function() {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should NOT trigger: callback receives the error value
export const shouldNotTriggerWithErrorParam = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch((cause) => Effect.succeed(cause))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch returning a different Effect constructor
export const shouldNotTriggerOtherEffect = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.sync(() => "fallback"))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch with logic other than just Effect.succeed
export const shouldNotTriggerWithLogic = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    console.log("recovering")
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed" ===

--- file:///.src/catchToOrElseSucceed.ts ---
import { Effect, pipe } from "effect"

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed
export const shouldTriggerArrow = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(42))
)

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed (block body)
export const shouldTriggerArrowBlock = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should trigger: pipe style
export const shouldTriggerPipe = pipe(
  Effect.fail("error"),
  Effect.orElseSucceed(() => "fallback")
)

// Should trigger: with function expression
export const shouldTriggerFunctionExpr = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(function() {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should NOT trigger: callback receives the error value
export const shouldNotTriggerWithErrorParam = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch((cause) => Effect.succeed(cause))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch returning a different Effect constructor
export const shouldNotTriggerOtherEffect = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.sync(() => "fallback"))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch with logic other than just Effect.succeed
export const shouldNotTriggerWithLogic = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    console.log("recovering")
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable catchToOrElseSucceed for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Replace with Effect.orElseSucceed" ===

--- file:///.src/catchToOrElseSucceed.ts ---
import { Effect, pipe } from "effect"

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed
export const shouldTriggerArrow = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed(42))
)

// Should trigger: catch with arrow function returning Effect.succeed (block body)
export const shouldTriggerArrowBlock = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)

// Should trigger: pipe style
export const shouldTriggerPipe = pipe(
  Effect.fail("error"),
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.succeed("fallback"))
)

// Should trigger: with function expression
export const shouldTriggerFunctionExpr = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.orElseSucceed(function() {
    return 42
  })
)

// Should NOT trigger: callback receives the error value
export const shouldNotTriggerWithErrorParam = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch((cause) => Effect.succeed(cause))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch returning a different Effect constructor
export const shouldNotTriggerOtherEffect = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => Effect.sync(() => "fallback"))
)

// Should NOT trigger: catch with logic other than just Effect.succeed
export const shouldNotTriggerWithLogic = Effect.fail("error").pipe(
  Effect.catch(() => {
    console.log("recovering")
    return Effect.succeed(42)
  })
)
